content: Hi, I'm trying to get back into a routine after a busy few weeks of game development. I've been putting in 12-hour days, 6 days a week.
That sounds exhausting! What have you been working on?

I've been developing a new indie game, and we're getting close to launch. I'm really excited about it, but it's been taking a toll on my personal life. I've barely had time for hiking or woodworking lately.
It's great that you're passionate about your work, but it's also important to make time for self-care and hobbies. Do you have any fun plans for the weekend?
I'll be volunteering at the local animal shelter on Saturday, which I always enjoy. And on Sunday, I'm planning to go mountain biking with some friends in the nearby trails.
That sounds like a great way to spend the weekend! It's wonderful that you're giving back to the community by volunteering at the animal shelter.
Yeah, I love animals, and it's a great way to meet like-minded people. I've even met some friends who share my passion for photography and drone photography.
That's terrific! It's always great to connect with people who share your interests. What kind of games do you enjoy developing?

I love working on puzzle games, and I'm really interested in experimenting with new mechanics and gameplay ideas. I've been inspired by some of the games I played at the Indie Game Festival in Los Angeles last year.
Puzzle games can be so much fun to play and develop. What's your favorite part about attending the festival?

I loved meeting other developers and learning about their projects. It was really inspiring to see so many creative and innovative games. I'm hoping to attend again next year and showcase my own game.
That sounds like a great experience. I'm sure you'll have a lot to showcase next year. Do you have any other upcoming events or projects that you're excited about?
